title: geissois ternata var. minor
intro: Geissois ternata var. minor, also known as the Yellow Maple, is a variety of small tree native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Geissois ternata var. minor include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Geissois ternata var. minor can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
